_meta.note: Rates verified May 2026 — check provider pricing pages for updates before next release

aws.name: AWS

compute.description: t3.micro EC2 (2 vCPU, 1 GB) — us-east-1 on-demand

bandwidth.description: Data transfer out to internet (first 15 GB/mo free)

aws.notes: Free tier is time-limited to 12 months. After that, standard on-demand rates apply.

gcp.name: GCP
gcp.notes: Always-free tier: one e2-micro instance per month forever (720 hrs). No free managed database.

azure.name: Azure

compute.description: B1s VM (1 vCPU, 1 GB) — East US

database.description: Azure DB for MySQL Flexible — Burstable B1ms

azure.notes: Free tier is time-limited to 12 months. After that, standard pay-as-you-go rates apply.

digitalocean.name: DigitalOcean

compute.description: Basic Droplet (1 vCPU, 1 GB RAM) — ~$6/mo (billed at 672 hrs/mo)

storage.description: Spaces Object Storage (after 250 GB free with $5/mo plan)

bandwidth.description: Bandwidth overage — 1 TB free/mo per Droplet

database.description: Managed PostgreSQL — Basic (1 GB RAM, 1 vCPU)

digitalocean.notes: No permanent free compute tier. New accounts receive $200 credit valid for 60 days.

render.name: Render

compute.description: Starter web service (512 MB RAM, 0.5 CPU) — $7/mo always-on

storage.description: Render Disks persistent block storage (effective rate for typical usage)

bandwidth.description: Outbound bandwidth overage after 100 GB free/month

database.description: Managed PostgreSQL Starter (256 MB RAM, 1 GB storage)

render.notes: Starter instance is $7/mo flat with no free compute hours. A separate Free instance type exists but spins down after 15 minutes of inactivity. Managed databases are free for 90 days, then $7/month.

netlify.name: Netlify

bandwidth.description: Bandwidth overage at $0.55/GB after 100 GB free/month

database.description: No native database — integrates with Supabase, PlanetScale, or Neon
database.note: Netlify does not offer a managed database — use PlanetScale, Supabase, or Neon separately

netlify.notes: Netlify is a serverless/edge platform — compute is invocation-based, not hour-based. 1 compute hour is modeled as ~72,000 invocations (50 ms avg execution). Database requires a third-party provider.

vercel.name: Vercel

compute.description: Serverless Functions — $0.0000002/invocation; Hobby plan includes functions

storage.description: Vercel Blob — after 1 GB free on Hobby

bandwidth.description: Bandwidth overage at $0.40/GB after 100 GB free

database.note: Vercel does not offer a managed database — use PlanetScale, Supabase, or Neon separately

vercel.notes: Vercel is a serverless/edge platform — compute is invocation-based, not hour-based. 1 compute hour is modeled as ~72,000 invocations (50 ms avg execution). Database services require third-party providers.

cloudflare.name: Cloudflare

storage.description: R2 Object Storage — after 10 GB free, zero egress fees

bandwidth.description: Zero egress fees from R2 — no data transfer charges

database.note: Cloudflare D1 is serverless SQLite — pricing is per query, not per instance

cloudflare.notes: Cloudflare charges zero egress fees on R2 — a major cost advantage over S3, GCP, and Azure. Compute (Workers) is invocation-based with no hourly pricing. Free tier: 100K requests/day (~3M/mo).
